comments.author: @mungnguyen1963
comments.text: The scene between 8 AM and 6 PM where everyone stops to listen to the national anthem reminds me of the first time I stood bewildered in the middle of an MRT station. Suddenly, I saw thousands of people standing still like they were playing hide-and-seek; I thought I'd accidentally stepped into a science fiction movie! 🤣

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: That is such a funny way to describe that experience, but it really is a surreal moment to witness for the first time! :)

comments.author: @GlobeLife.Documentary
comments.text: The "Mai Pen Rai" philosophy is honestly the best mindset for anyone living in SE Asia. It’s saved my sanity so many times living here!

comments.author: @NICEGLOBE05
comments.text: Thailand is such a fascinating country of contrasts! In the morning, you see people quietly giving alms to monks and worshipping at sacred temples, and by midnight, the streets transform into the most energetic party hubs on Earth. Thai people have this incredible ability to enjoy life to the fullest without losing their spiritual roots. Awesome travel documentary!

comments.author: @GlobalCountryExplorer
comments.text: Thailand is far more than its nightlife. The warmth of its people, rich traditions, and deep cultural roots are what leave the strongest impression on me after documentaries like this.

replies.author: @AmazingGlobalDiscoveries
replies.text: Greetings to Canada! We hope you're enjoying the video. 😊

comments.author: @AmazingDiscoveriesTopUS
comments.text: Thailand is famous for whild nightlife... but 90% of the  population is Buddhist. Which side is the "real"Thai Land?

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: The cultural research in this documentary is outstanding. Breaking down the Thai smile into a language of "Kreng Jai" (the reluctance to impose) [05:10] rather than just pure happiness is a nuance most travel videos completely miss. Coupling that with the philosophy of "Mai Pen Rai" as a way to hold the world more loosely [10:42] really explains how a country with such wild, contrasting day and night lives can remain so profoundly peaceful and safe. 10/10 scriptwriting! 👏
